headline: Tesla Q1 2026 BEAT: Revenue $22.38B (+16% YoY), non-GAAP EPS $0.41 (+52% YoY) vs $0.37 consensus, gross margin 21.1% (+478 bps YoY) — strongest margin in years; TSLA +4% AH; partly aided by one-time warranty/tariff benefits

⚠️ Tesla quietly slips 5-city robotaxi 1H 2026 timelines — Phoenix, Miami, Orlando, Tampa, Las Vegas now listed as 'preparations underway' with no firm date in Q1 deck; previously committed to 1H 2026 for all 7 cities; only Dallas + Houston confirmed live
source: Electrek / Business Insider / Tesla Q1 Shareholder Deck

headline: Tesla files permits for first robotaxi-only Supercharger stations in Arizona — dedicated V4 fleet charging hubs not open to public; first physical autonomous fleet infrastructure, sited in Waymo's home market

Forbes: Tesla targeting one Cybercab every 10 seconds at full Giga Texas scale — annual capacity of 2 million units; production ramp started April 2026 with Cybercab on AI4.5 hardware (AI5 pushed to 2027)

Optimus Gen 3 enters mass production — Fremont factory floor converted for 1M units/year long-term capacity; GigaShanghai confirmed as second production site; Gen 3 powered by FSD AI; internal factory deployment before external sales

FSD v14.3 MLIR rewrite confirmed: 20% faster reaction times via rebuilt AI compiler; new fleet-to-fleet RL pipeline sourcing 'hard examples' from global fleet; v14.3.1 patch rolling fleet-wide on 2026.2.9.7 build

Dallas/Houston robotaxi fleet expands to 4 unsupervised vehicles after first geofence expansion — early-stage small coverage zones; Tesla total cross-fleet unsupervised vehicles: 17 (Austin 13, Dallas 2, Houston 2)

Samsung Taylor Texas 2nm fab kicks off equipment installation April 24 — AI5 chip production milestone; $16.5B Tesla deal signed Jul 2025; 2nm yields in high-50% range; volume production targeting 2027 for Optimus and supercomputer clusters
